name: Rodeo a la Sierra de la Panamera — circular por el Puerto de Navalmoral
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Casas Del Puerto are characterized by the mountainous terrain of the Sierra de Gredos, featuring significant elevation changes and challenging climbs. The region offers a landscape of high mountain passes and deep valleys, providing a demanding environment for road cyclists. Routes frequently traverse passes like Peñanegra, Chía, and Alto del Reventón, demanding sustained effort and technical skill. This area is defined by its rugged natural features and the distinct challenges presented by its elevation profile. Best road cycling routes around Casas Del Puerto The most popular road cycling route is Puerto de Peñanegra y Chia desde San Martin - Parque regional Sierra de Gredos — circular desde Piedrahíta, a 51.2 miles (82.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 41 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial climbs through the Sierra de Gredos mountain range. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Peña Negra and Chía Pass Loop from Piedrahíta, a difficult 40.7 miles (65.5 km) path. This loop offers a challenging ride through two prominent mountain passes, providing continuous ascents and descents. Local road cyclists also love the Puertos de Peñanegra y Alto del Reventón — circular desde Piedrahíta, a 50.1 miles (80.7 km) trail leading through multiple mountain passes, often completed in about 4 hours 16 minutes. Road cycling around Casas Del Puerto is defined by high mountain passes, challenging climbs, and scenic valleys within the Sierra de Gredos. The network offers options primarily for experienced cyclists due to the demanding elevation profiles. The routes in Casas Del Puerto are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from more than 4 reviews. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Casas Del Puerto's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Casas Del Puerto?
A: Road cycling routes around Casas Del Puerto are defined by the mountainous terrain of the Sierra de Gredos. You can expect significant elevation changes and challenging climbs, traversing high mountain passes like Peñanegra, Chía, and Alto del Reventón, and deep valleys. The landscape offers a demanding environment for experienced road cyclists.

Q: How difficult are the road cycling routes near Casas Del Puerto?
A: The road cycling routes in Casas Del Puerto are generally considered difficult, featuring substantial climbs and continuous ascents and descents through the Sierra de Gredos mountain range. They are primarily suited for experienced cyclists due to the demanding elevation profiles.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Casas Del Puerto?
A: There are 5 dedicated road cycling routes available around Casas Del Puerto, all of which are rated as difficult. These routes offer varied challenges across the Sierra de Gredos.

Q: Are there any circular road bike routes in Casas Del Puerto?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Casas Del Puerto are circular. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063028971/puerto-de-penanegra-y-chia-desde-san-martin-parque-regional-sierra-de-gredos-circular-desde-piedrahita" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Peñanegra y Chia desde San Martin - Parque regional Sierra de Gredos — circular desde Piedrahíta&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1315444519/pena-negra-and-chia-pass-loop-from-piedrahita" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Negra and Chía Pass Loop from Piedrahíta&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062415266/puertos-de-penanegra-y-alto-del-reventon-circular-desde-piedrahita" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puertos de Peñanegra y Alto del Reventón — circular desde Piedrahíta&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some of the longer road cycling routes in the region?
A: For longer rides, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063028971/puerto-de-penanegra-y-chia-desde-san-martin-parque-regional-sierra-de-gredos-circular-desde-piedrahita" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Peñanegra y Chia desde San Martin - Parque regional Sierra de Gredos — circular desde Piedrahíta&lt;/a&gt;, which covers approximately 82.4 km (51.2 miles) with over 1800 meters of elevation gain. Another substantial option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062415266/puertos-de-penanegra-y-alto-del-reventon-circular-desde-piedrahita" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puertos de Peñanegra y Alto del Reventón — circular desde Piedrahíta&lt;/a&gt;, spanning about 80.7 km (50.1 miles).

Q: What landmarks or viewpoints can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Along the road cycling routes, you'll encounter several notable landmarks and viewpoints. These include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1711130" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chía Pass (1,663 m)&lt;/a&gt;, the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6905909" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Peña Negra&lt;/a&gt;, and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4448740" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Bonilla de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass through the settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5585743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villatoro&lt;/a&gt; and see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4699488" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rollo de Muñotello&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Casas Del Puerto?
A: The road cycling experience in Casas Del Puerto is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the challenging climbs through the Sierra de Gredos and the scenic high mountain passes, which offer a rewarding and demanding ride.

Q: Are there any routes that combine multiple mountain passes?
A: Yes, several routes are designed to take you over multiple mountain passes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062415266/puertos-de-penanegra-y-alto-del-reventon-circular-desde-piedrahita" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puertos de Peñanegra y Alto del Reventón — circular desde Piedrahíta&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, leading through both Peñanegra and Alto del Reventón passes. Similarly, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063028971/puerto-de-penanegra-y-chia-desde-san-martin-parque-regional-sierra-de-gredos-circular-desde-piedrahita" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Peñanegra y Chia desde San Martin - Parque regional Sierra de Gredos — circular desde Piedrahíta&lt;/a&gt; covers both Peñanegra and Chía passes.

Q: What is the average duration for a road cycling route in Casas Del Puerto?
A: Given the challenging terrain and significant distances, the average duration for road cycling routes in Casas Del Puerto ranges from approximately 3 hours 30 minutes to 4 hours 45 minutes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063028971/puerto-de-penanegra-y-chia-desde-san-martin-parque-regional-sierra-de-gredos-circular-desde-piedrahita" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Peñanegra y Chia desde San Martin&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 4 hours 41 minutes.

Q: Can I find routes that explore specific valleys or regions within Sierra de Gredos?
A: Absolutely. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1315453509/chia-pass-and-corneja-valley-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chía Pass and Corneja Valley Loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlight the Corneja Valley, offering a distinct regional experience alongside the mountain passes.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Serrota summit?
A: Yes, some routes in the area provide views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5539755" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serrota summit&lt;/a&gt;. While not directly on every route, the mountainous landscape of Sierra de Gredos often offers glimpses of prominent peaks like Serrota.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking near Casas Del Puerto?
A: Given the high mountain passes and potential for challenging weather, the best time for road biking in the Sierra de Gredos region around Casas Del Puerto is typically during the warmer months, from late spring to early autumn. This avoids the snow and ice that can affect higher elevations in winter and early spring.
tips: From the same road you can see the town that is located higher up the Sierra de la Panamera mountain, Sotalbo. In addition, on the south side and on one of the slopes, there is the Castro de Ulaca, an old castle.

This is one of the ports that was best known for world cycling at the end of the 20th century for the Vuelta España. But the port was reformed years ago and nowadays it is more open and where there are windy days, it is an important factor.
Perfect Area for a little break

It is 1,663 meters above sea level and connects the Corneja Valley (Valdecorneja) with the Alto Valle del Alberche through a local road that connects the towns of Navacepedilla de Corneja and San Martín de la Vega del Alberche. It separates the Sierra de Villafranca and the massif of La Serrota.
